Professor View -- / --
Travel English -- 32 Aulas

Elaine Mieko Pinho

De viajante insegura para viajante independente e confiante em 32 aulas

A2 Indaiatuba, SP Advogada / Administradora de Empresa 60 min / Online / 2x semana
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Elaine Mieko Pinho
61 anos
Advogada / Administradora de Empresa
Indaiatuba, SP
A2 (Elementar)
Travel English
32 aulas de 60 min
Online (Zoom) / 2x por semana

Jornada de Transformação

De: Viajante insegura que depende de outras pessoas para se comunicar em inglês no exterior

Para: Viajante independente e confiante, capaz de resolver situações reais de viagem sozinha em 32 aulas

Promessa do Programa

"De 'me sinto muito insegura' para 'eu não desisto -- e agora eu consigo.'"

Forças

  • Persistência -- não desiste facilmente
  • Auto-monitoramento -- sabe onde erra
  • Clareza diagnóstica -- identifica suas dificuldades
  • Vocabulário de busca ativa -- pesquisa por conta própria
  • Engajamento social -- gosta de interagir com pessoas

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Pronúncia e entonação -- dificuldade com sons do inglês
  • Vocabulário de viagem -- limitado para situações reais
  • Fluência oral -- demora para formular frases
  • Experiência com aula individual -- primeira vez em formato 1:1
  • Confiança ao falar -- sente-se insegura ao se expressar

Currículo -- 32 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
1Diagnostic + Who Is ElainePresent simple + "I usually/want to/am not sure"Self-introduction role-playRecord 1-min self-introduction
2At the Airport -- Check-inCould I...? / I would like...Airport check-in simulationWrite airport phrases
3On the Plane -- RequestsCould I have...? / Excuse me...In-flight request practiceListen to flight announcements
4Immigration and CustomsPresent simple questions + short answersImmigration interview role-playPractice customs Q&A
5Taxis and RideshareDirections + imperativesUber/taxi simulationWrite directions to hotel
6Hotel Check-inReservations + polite requestsHotel front desk role-playRecord hotel check-in dialogue
7Hotel ProblemsThere is/are + complaintsProblem-solving simulationWrite complaint phrases
8Review 1 -- Airport to HotelAll structures (Lessons 1-7)Full journey simulationReflection audio
9Restaurant BasicsI would like + orderingRestaurant role-playPractice menu reading
10Special Requests -- AllergiesI am allergic to + Can you...?Allergy scenario simulationWrite allergy card
11Paying the BillNumbers + Could I have the check?Payment simulationPractice tip calculations
12Coffee Shops and CafesOrdering + small talkCafe role-playRecord coffee order
13Asking for DirectionsWhere is...? + prepositionsDirection-giving practiceMap exercise
14Public TransportationWhich + How do I get to...?Metro/bus simulationPlan a route
15Shopping -- StoresHow much + Do you have...?Shopping simulationWrite shopping list phrases
16Review 2 -- Food and Getting AroundAll structures (Lessons 9-15)Full day simulationReflection audio
17Sightseeing -- Tickets and ToursI would like to + When does...?Tour booking simulationResearch a tour online
18Taking Photos -- Asking StrangersExcuse me, could you...?Photo request practiceRecord photo request phrases
19Small Talk -- Meeting PeopleQuestions + present simpleConversation practiceWrite 5 small talk questions
20Phone Calls -- ReservationsI am calling to + spelling namePhone call simulationPractice phone reservation
21At the PharmacyI need + symptoms vocabularyPharmacy role-playWrite symptom phrases
22Minor Emergencies -- Losing ThingsI lost + past simpleLost item simulationWrite emergency phrases
23At the Doctor -- SymptomsI have + body parts + symptomsDoctor visit role-playRecord symptom description
24Review 3 -- Daily Life AbroadAll structures (Lessons 17-23)Full scenario simulationReflection audio
25Weather and ClothingIt is + adjectives + shouldWeather discussion practiceDescribe weather in 5 cities
26Cultural Differences -- Being PolitePlease/Thank you + Would you mind...?Politeness scenariosCompare BR vs US politeness
27Souvenirs and GiftsI am looking for + comparativesGift shopping simulationPlan gift shopping list
28Hotel Check-outCould you + review expressionsCheck-out simulationWrite check-out phrases
29Airport Return -- Going HomeAll airport vocabulary reviewReturn journey simulationRecord airport dialogue
30Telling Travel StoriesPast simple + sequencingStory-telling practiceWrite travel story
31Review 4 -- Full SimulationAll structures integratedFull trip simulationRecord full introduction + story
32Final Simulation -- Elaine's Solo TripAll structures integrated45-min immersion simulationFinal reflection audio

Welcome, Elaine!

Onboarding

Bem-vinda ao programa! Antes de cada aula, complete as atividades de Pre-class. Aqui estão 5 frases de emergência que você pode usar a qualquer momento:

1 Could you repeat that, please? Poderia repetir, por favor?
2 I am not sure I understand. Could you explain? Não tenho certeza se entendi. Poderia explicar?
3 Could you speak more slowly, please? Poderia falar mais devagar, por favor?
4 I am not sure how to say this in English. Não tenho certeza de como dizer isso em inglês.
5 Thank you for your patience. Obrigada pela paciência.
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Who Is Elaine?

Diagnostic + Talking About Yourself: Present simple & travel expressions
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Trip -- a journey to a place
"I usually travel with my family on every trip." (Eu geralmente viajo com minha família em cada viagem.)
Abroad -- in a foreign country
"I want to feel more confident when I travel abroad." (Quero me sentir mais confiante quando viajo para o exterior.)
Alone -- without other people
"I want to travel alone and feel confident." (Quero viajar sozinha e me sentir confiante.)
Confident -- feeling sure about yourself
"I want to feel confident at the airport." (Quero me sentir confiante no aeroporto.)
Nervous -- worried or anxious
"I feel nervous when I need to speak English." (Fico nervosa quando preciso falar inglês.)
Manage -- to be in charge of something
"I manage a company with my family." (Eu administro uma empresa com minha família.)
Check In -- to register at a hotel or airport
"I want to check in at a hotel by myself." (Quero fazer check-in num hotel sozinha.)
Deal With -- to handle a problem or situation
"I need to learn how to deal with problems when I travel." (Preciso aprender a lidar com problemas quando viajo.)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition in English.

Trip
Abroad
Alone
Confident
Nervous
Manage
Check In
Deal With

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Elaine lives in Indaiatuba, a small city in São Paulo. She works as a lawyer and she manages a family company. Elaine loves to travel, but she feels nervous when she needs to speak English abroad. She usually travels with her family. She wants to travel alone and feel confident. She is not sure how to check in at hotels or deal with problems by herself. But Elaine does not give up. She studies English every week because she wants to be an independent traveler.

1. Where does Elaine live?
A She lives in São Paulo city.
B She lives in Indaiatuba, a small city in São Paulo.
C She lives in New York.
2. How does Elaine feel when she speaks English abroad?
A She feels confident and happy.
B She feels nervous.
C She feels alone.
3. What does Elaine want to do?
A She wants to stop traveling.
B She wants to manage a hotel.
C She wants to travel alone and feel confident.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple = facts, habits, routines (fatos, hábitos, rotinas)
Usamos para falar sobre coisas que fazemos regularmente ou que são verdade sobre nós.

FormStructureExample
AffirmativeI + verb (base)"I work as a lawyer." / "I live in Indaiatuba."
NegativeI + do not + verb"I do not speak English well."
QuestionDo + I/you + verb?"Do you travel alone?"

Expressões úteis para viagem:

  • "I usually..." = Eu geralmente... (hábito) -- "I usually travel with my family."
  • "I want to..." = Eu quero... (desejo) -- "I want to feel confident."
  • "I am not sure how to say..." = Não tenho certeza de como dizer... -- "I am not sure how to say this."

Common Mistake:

"I no speak English."

"I do not speak English well."

Em inglês, usamos "do not" (ou "don't") + verbo base para negativas no present simple. Nunca "no" antes do verbo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I usually with my family."
"I want to feel more when I travel abroad."
"I feel when I need to speak English."
"I a company with my family."
"I want to at a hotel by myself."
"I need to learn how to problems when I travel."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? "I usually travel with my family."
? "I want to travel alone and feel confident."
? "My name is Elaine. I am from Brazil."
? "I feel nervous when I speak English abroad."
? "I am a lawyer and I manage a company."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

My name is Elaine. I am from Brazil.
I am a lawyer and I manage a company.
I love to travel, but I feel nervous when I need to speak English.
I want to travel alone and feel confident.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You arrive at a hotel. What do you say?
A "I want room now."
B "I would like to check in, please. My name is Elaine Pinho."
C "Give me a room."
2. Someone asks: "Where are you from?" What is the best response?
A "Brazil people."
B "I from Brazil."
C "I am from Brazil. I live in a small city called Indaiatuba."
3. You do not understand what someone said. What do you say?
A "I am not sure I understand. Could you repeat that, please?"
B "What? Say again."
C "I no understand nothing."
4. Your hotel room has a problem. What do you say?
A "Room bad! Fix!"
B "Excuse me, I need to deal with a problem with my room."
C "I am nervous about the room."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine you are at a hotel lobby in New York. The receptionist asks: "Welcome! Could you tell me about yourself?" Record your answer in English. Try to include: your name, where you are from, what you do, and why you are traveling.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1 My name is Elaine. I am from Brazil. Meu nome e Elaine. Eu sou do Brasil.
2 I work as a lawyer and I manage a company. Trabalho como advogada e administro uma empresa.
3 I usually travel with my family, but I want to be independent. Geralmente viajo com minha família, mas quero ser independente.
4 I feel nervous abroad, but I am learning. Fico nervosa no exterior, mas estou aprendendo.
5 I want to check in at hotels and deal with problems by myself. Quero fazer check-in em hoteis e resolver problemas sozinha.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

At the Airport -- Check-in and Boarding

"Could I...?" for polite requests + "Where is/are...?" for asking locations
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Boarding Pass -- the card you need to get on the plane
"Could I see your boarding pass, please?" (Cartão de embarque)
Gate -- the door area where you wait for your flight
"The flight departs from Gate 14." (Portão de embarque)
Luggage -- bags and suitcases you take when you travel
"Could I check in two pieces of luggage?" (Bagagem)
Passport -- the official document for international travel
"Please have your passport ready." (Passaporte)
Departure -- when a plane leaves
"The departure time is 10:30 AM." (Partida/saída)
Arrival -- when a plane gets to a destination
"What is the arrival time in New York?" (Chegada)
Seat -- the place where you sit on the plane
"Could I have a window seat, please?" (Assento)
Delay -- when something happens later than planned
"There is a two-hour delay on our flight." (Atraso)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition in English.

Boarding Pass
Gate
Luggage
Passport
Departure
Arrival
Seat
Delay

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Elaine arrives at Guarulhos Airport in São Paulo. She walks to the check-in counter with her luggage. The attendant asks for her passport and boarding pass. Elaine feels a little nervous, but she remembers her English. "Could I have a window seat, please?" she asks. The attendant smiles and says, "Of course! Your departure is at Gate 14. The flight departs at 10:30 AM." Elaine looks at the departure board. She sees her flight. "Where is Gate 14?" she asks a staff member. "Go straight and turn left after the coffee shop," he says. Elaine finds the gate and waits. She hears an announcement: "Flight AA 402 is now boarding." Elaine stands up, takes her boarding pass and passport, and walks to the gate. She feels confident. This is her first flight alone, and she is ready.

1. What does Elaine ask the attendant for?
A She asks for an aisle seat.
B She asks for a window seat.
C She asks for a first-class seat.
2. Where is Elaine's departure gate?
A Gate 12.
B Gate 7.
C Gate 14.
3. How does Elaine feel at the end?
A She feels nervous and scared.
B She feels confident and ready.
C She feels tired and wants to go home.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

"Could I...?" = polite request (Pedido educado)
Usamos "Could I" + verbo base + complemento para fazer pedidos educados em inglês.

Could I+ base verb+ complement?
Could Ihavea window seat? = Posso ter um assento na janela?
Could Icheck inmy luggage? = Posso despachar minha bagagem?
Could Iseethe departure board? = Posso ver o painel de partidas?

"Where is/are...?" = asking for locations (Perguntando sobre localizações)
Usamos "Where is" para coisas no singular e "Where are" para coisas no plural.

  • "Where is Gate 14?" = Onde é o portão 14?
  • "Where are the restrooms?" = Onde ficam os banheiros?

Common Mistake:

"Where the gate is?"

"Where is the gate?"

Em inglês, nas perguntas com "Where", o verbo vem ANTES do sujeito: "Where is + noun?" Nunca "Where + noun + is?"

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word.

" I have an aisle seat, please?"
"Where is the for Flight 402?"
"Please have your and boarding pass ready."
"There is a one-hour on our flight."
"The time is 3:15 PM."
" I check in three pieces of ?"

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these airport steps in the correct order.

? "You receive your boarding pass and check in your luggage."
? "You hear the boarding announcement and get on the plane."
? "You arrive at the airport with your luggage."
? "You go through security and find your gate."
? "You go to the check-in counter and show your passport."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

Could I have a window seat, please?
Where is Gate 14?
There is a two-hour delay on our flight.
Please have your passport and boarding pass ready.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You want a specific seat on the plane. What do you say?
A "I want window. Give me."
B "Could I have a window seat, please?"
C "Window seat now."
2. You cannot find your gate at the airport. What do you say?
A "Where the gate is?"
B "Gate where?"
C "Excuse me, where is Gate 14?"
3. Your flight is delayed. What do you ask?
A "Is there a delay on this flight?"
B "Why plane no go?"
C "The delay where is?"
4. You want to check in your bags. What do you say?
A "Take my bags."
B "Bags here, please."
C "Could I check in my luggage here?"

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine you are at Guarulhos Airport. You need to check in for your flight to New York. What do you say to the attendant? Record your answer in English. Try to include: a polite request for your seat, asking about the gate, and checking in your luggage.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1 Could I have a window seat, please? Posso ter um assento na janela?
2 Where is Gate 14? Onde é o portão 14?
3 Is there a delay on this flight? Há atraso neste voo?
4 Could I check in my luggage here? Posso despachar minha bagagem aqui?
5 What time does the flight depart? A que horas o voo parte?
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

On the Plane -- Requests and Comfort

"Could I have...?" + "I would like..." for in-flight requests
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Flight Attendant -- the person who helps passengers on the plane
"The flight attendant helped me with my bag." (Comissário/comissária de bordo)
Blanket -- a cover to keep warm
"Could I have a blanket, please?" (Cobertor)
Pillow -- a soft object for your head
"I need a pillow to rest my head." (Travesseiro)
Tray Table -- the small table attached to the seat in front of you
"Please open your tray table for the meal." (Mesinha de bordo)
Fasten -- to close or attach securely
"Please fasten your seatbelt for takeoff." (Apertar/prender)
Seatbelt -- the safety belt you wear on the plane
"The seatbelt sign is on. Please remain seated." (Cinto de segurança)
Aisle -- the passage between rows of seats
"Excuse me, could I get through to the aisle?" (Corredor)
Overhead Bin -- the storage space above your seat
"Please put your bag in the overhead bin." (Compartimento superior)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition in English.

Flight Attendant
Blanket
Pillow
Tray Table
Fasten
Seatbelt
Aisle
Overhead Bin

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Elaine is on her flight to New York. She finds her window seat and puts her bag in the overhead bin. A flight attendant walks by. "Could I have a blanket and a pillow, please?" Elaine asks. "Of course!" he says. "My name is Mark. Let me know if you need anything."

The captain speaks: "Please fasten your seatbelts." Elaine fastens her seatbelt and opens her tray table. She would like something to drink. When Mark comes back, she says, "I would like some orange juice, please." "Excellent choice!" Mark says.

During the flight, Elaine needs to go to the restroom. She asks the passenger next to her, "Excuse me, could I get through to the aisle?" The passenger smiles and stands up. Elaine feels proud. She is communicating in English on a plane -- alone and confident.

1. What does Elaine ask the flight attendant for?
A A window seat and a passport
B A blanket and a pillow
C A boarding pass and luggage
2. What does the captain ask passengers to do?
A Open the overhead bin
B Close the tray table
C Fasten the seatbelts
3. How does Elaine ask for orange juice?
A "I want orange juice."
B "I would like some orange juice, please."
C "Give me orange juice."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

"Could I have...?" = pedir um objeto educadamente (requesting an object politely)

"I would like..." = expressar uma preferência educadamente (expressing a preference politely)

StructureExampleTradução
Could I have + noun + ?Could I have a blanket, please?Posso ter um cobertor, por favor?
I would like + noun + , please.I would like some water, please.Eu gostaria de um pouco de água, por favor.
Could you + verb + ?Could you help me with the overhead bin?Poderia me ajudar com o compartimento?

Common Mistake / Erro Comum:

"I want coffee." ✗ → "I would like a coffee, please." ✓

"I want" soa direto demais em inglês. Use "I would like" para ser educado. / "I want" sounds too direct in English. Use "I would like" to be polite.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete the sentences with the correct word.

" a blanket, please?"
"I some orange juice, please."
"Please your seatbelt for takeoff."
"Excuse me, could I get through to the ?"
"Please put your bag in the ."
"The helped me with my bag."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these actions in the correct order for being on a plane.

? Ask the flight attendant for food or drinks.
? Sit down and fasten your seatbelt.
? Close your tray table and fasten your seatbelt for landing.
? Find your seat and put your bag in the overhead bin.
? Open your tray table when the crew says it is safe.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

Could I have a blanket and a pillow, please?
I would like some orange juice, please.
Excuse me, could I get through to the aisle?
Please fasten your seatbelt for takeoff.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

1. You are cold on the plane. What do you say to the flight attendant?
A "I want blanket now."
B "Could I have a blanket, please?"
C "Where is the blanket?"
2. The captain says: "Please fasten your seatbelts." What should you do?
A Open the tray table
B Put your bag in the overhead bin
C Close and secure your seatbelt
3. You want to order a drink. How do you ask politely?
A "Give me water."
B "I want water."
C "I would like some water, please."
4. You need to go to the restroom but the person next to you is blocking the way. What do you say?
A "Excuse me, could I get through to the aisle?"
B "Move, please."
C "I want to go to the restroom."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection
You are on a long flight. The flight attendant asks what you need. Record yourself ordering food, asking for a blanket, and requesting help with the overhead bin.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1 Could I have a blanket and a pillow, please? Posso ter um cobertor e um travesseiro, por favor?
2 I would like some water, please. Eu gostaria de um pouco de água, por favor.
3 Excuse me, could I get through to the aisle? Com licença, posso passar para o corredor?
4 Where is the overhead bin for my bag? Onde é o compartimento para minha mala?
5 Could you help me fasten my seatbelt? Poderia me ajudar a apertar meu cinto?
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Arriving -- Immigration and Customs

"I am here to..." (purpose) + "I am staying for..." (duration) + "I have / I do not have..." (declarations)
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Immigration -- the place where officers check your passport when you arrive
"The immigration officer checked my passport." (Imigração)
Customs -- the place where officers check your luggage for restricted items
"You need to go through customs after immigration." (Alfândega)
Declaration -- an official statement about what you are bringing into the country
"I filled out the declaration form on the plane." (Declaração)
Purpose -- the reason for doing something
"What is the purpose of your visit?" (Propósito/objetivo)
Vacation -- a time for rest and travel, not work
"I am here on vacation for two weeks." (Férias)
Return -- to go back to where you came from
"When is your return flight?" (Retorno/voltar)
Declare -- to officially say what you have or are bringing
"I have nothing to declare." (Declarar)
Officer -- a person with authority, like a police officer or immigration official
"The officer asked me about my luggage." (Oficial/agente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition.

Immigration
Customs
Declaration
Purpose
Vacation
Return
Declare
Officer

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine's plane lands at JFK Airport in New York. She follows the signs to immigration. There is a long line, but Elaine is ready. An officer calls her: "Next, please." Elaine walks to the counter and gives him her passport.

"What is the purpose of your visit?" the officer asks. "I am here on vacation," Elaine says with a smile. "How long are you staying?" "I am staying for two weeks." "Where are you staying?" "I am staying at a hotel in Manhattan." The officer stamps her passport: "Welcome to the United States."

Elaine feels proud. She walks to customs. A sign says: "Do you have anything to declare?" Elaine reads her declaration form. She has nothing to declare. She walks through the green lane and picks up her luggage. She is officially in New York -- alone and confident.

1. What is the purpose of Elaine's visit?
A She is here for work.
B She is here on vacation.
C She is here to study English.
2. How long is Elaine staying?
A One week.
B Two weeks.
C One month.
3. Does Elaine have anything to declare at customs?
A No, she has nothing to declare.
B Yes, she has food to declare.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
Stating Purpose: "I am here to..." / "I am here on..."

Inglês: Use "I am here to + verb" or "I am here on + noun" to say WHY you are visiting.

Português: Use "I am here to + verbo" ou "I am here on + substantivo" para dizer POR QUE você está visitando.

TypeEnglishPortuguês
Purpose (verb)I am here to visit my friend.Estou aqui para visitar meu amigo.
Purpose (noun)I am here on vacation.Estou aqui de férias.
Purpose (noun)I am here for tourism.Estou aqui a turismo.
Stating Duration: "I am staying for..."

Inglês: Use "I am staying for + time period" to say HOW LONG.

Português: Use "I am staying for + período" para dizer POR QUANTO TEMPO.

EnglishPortuguês
I am staying for two weeks.Vou ficar por duas semanas.
I am staying for ten days.Vou ficar por dez dias.
Customs Declarations: "I have / I do not have..."
EnglishPortuguês
I have nothing to declare.Não tenho nada a declarar.
I do not have any prohibited items.Não tenho nenhum item proibido.

Common Mistake:

"I come for vacation." ✗

"I am here on vacation." ✓

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"What is the of your visit?"
"I am here on ."
"I am for two weeks."
"I have nothing to ."
"The asked me about my luggage."
"She filled out the form on the plane."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? You answer the officer's questions about your visit.
? You arrive at immigration and wait in line.
? You pick up your luggage and exit the airport.
? The officer calls you and asks for your passport.
? You go through customs and walk through the green lane.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

I am here on vacation.
I am staying for two weeks.
I have nothing to declare.
What is the purpose of your visit?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

The immigration officer asks: "What is the purpose of your visit?" What do you say?
A "I come for vacation."
B "I am here on vacation."
C "Vacation."
The officer asks: "How long are you staying?" What do you say?
A "Two weeks long."
B "I stay two weeks."
C "I am staying for two weeks."
At customs, a sign says: "Do you have anything to declare?" You have no restricted items. What do you say?
A "I have nothing to declare."
B "I not have declare."
C "No declare."
The officer speaks too fast and you do not understand. What do you say?
A "What?"
B "Could you repeat that, please?"
C "I no understand."

Stage 5: Reflection

Reflection
Imagine you just landed at JFK Airport in New York. The immigration officer asks you: "What is the purpose of your visit? How long are you staying? Where are you staying?" Record your answers in English. Try to use: "I am here on/to...", "I am staying for...", and "I am staying at..."

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1 I am here on vacation. Estou aqui de férias.
2 I am staying for two weeks. Vou ficar por duas semanas.
3 I have nothing to declare. Não tenho nada a declarar.
4 I am staying at a hotel in Manhattan. Estou hospedada em um hotel em Manhattan.
5 Could you repeat that, please? I did not understand. Poderia repetir, por favor? Não entendi.
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

Getting Around -- Taxis and Rideshare

"Could you take me to...?" (requesting destinations) + "How much is it to...?" (asking prices) + "Turn left/right, go straight" (basic directions)
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Driver -- the person who drives the taxi or car
"The driver took me to the hotel." (Motorista)
Fare -- the price you pay for a taxi or bus ride
"The fare is about 70 dollars plus tolls." (Tarifa)
Destination -- the place you are going to
"What is your destination?" (Destino)
Traffic -- many cars on the road moving slowly
"The traffic is heavy today." (Trânsito)
Drop Off -- to leave someone at a place
"Could you drop me off at the main entrance?" (Deixar/descer)
Pick Up -- to collect someone from a place
"I will pick you up at 8 AM." (Buscar/pegar)
Meter -- the device in a taxi that shows the fare
"The meter shows 72 dollars." (Taxímetro)
Receipt -- a paper that shows how much you paid
"Could I have a receipt, please?" (Recibo)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition.

Driver
Fare
Destination
Traffic
Drop Off
Pick Up
Meter
Receipt

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine walks out of JFK Airport with her luggage. She sees a line of yellow taxis and a sign for rideshare pick up. She decides to take a taxi. "Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel in Manhattan, please?" she asks the driver. "Sure! My name is Miguel. It is about 45 minutes with traffic," he says. "How much is it to Manhattan?" Elaine asks. "The fare is about 70 dollars plus tolls," Miguel explains.

Elaine gets in and watches the meter. The traffic is heavy, but Miguel knows the way. "Turn right here and then go straight," he tells himself. After 40 minutes, they arrive. "Here we are. The Hilton Hotel. Could you drop me off at the main entrance, please?" Elaine says. Miguel stops the car. The meter shows 72 dollars. Elaine pays and asks for a receipt. "Thank you, Miguel! You were very kind." She picks up her luggage and walks into the hotel -- ready for her New York adventure.

1. How does Elaine ask the driver where she wants to go?
A "I want to go Manhattan."
B "Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel in Manhattan, please?"
C "Manhattan hotel go please."
2. How does Elaine ask about the price?
A "How much costs to Manhattan?"
B "What price Manhattan?"
C "How much is it to Manhattan?"
3. What does Elaine ask for before leaving the taxi?
A A blanket
B A receipt
C A declaration form

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
"Could you take me to...?" = pedindo um destino educadamente
EnglishPortuguês
Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel, please?Poderia me levar ao Hotel Hilton, por favor?
Could you take me to Times Square?Poderia me levar à Times Square?
Could you take me to the airport?Poderia me levar ao aeroporto?
"How much is it to...?" = perguntando o preço da corrida
EnglishPortuguês
How much is it to Manhattan?Quanto custa até Manhattan?
How much is it to the airport?Quanto custa até o aeroporto?
"Go straight / Turn left / Turn right" = direções básicas
EnglishPortuguês
Go straight and turn left at the traffic light.Siga reto e vire à esquerda no semáforo.
Turn right here and then go straight.Vire à direita aqui e depois siga reto.

Common Mistake:

"How much costs?" ✗

"How much is it to Manhattan?" ✓

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel, please?" Elaine asks the .
"How much is it to Manhattan?" -- "The is about 70 dollars plus tolls."
The is heavy, but Miguel knows the way.
"Could you me at the main entrance, please?"
The shows 72 dollars.
Elaine pays and asks for a .

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? You watch the meter during the ride.
? You exit the airport and see taxis outside.
? You pay the fare and ask for a receipt.
? You tell the driver your destination.
? You arrive and ask the driver to drop you off.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel, please?
How much is it to Manhattan?
Go straight and turn left at the traffic light.
Could I have a receipt, please?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You are at the airport and want to take a taxi to your hotel. What do you say to the driver?
A "Go hotel now."
B "Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel, please?"
C "I need go hotel."
You want to know the price before getting in the taxi. What do you ask?
A "How much costs?"
B "What price taxi?"
C "How much is it to Manhattan?"
You arrive at your hotel. How do you ask the driver to stop?
A "Could you drop me off here, please?"
B "Stop car here."
C "I want exit."
After paying the taxi fare, what do you ask for?
A "Give me paper."
B "Could I have a receipt, please?"
C "Where is the declaration?"

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you arrive at JFK Airport in New York. You need to get to your hotel in Manhattan. Describe what you would say to the taxi driver: your destination, ask about the fare, and ask for a receipt at the end.
Sugestão: "Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel in Manhattan, please? How much is it to Manhattan? ... Thank you! Could I have a receipt, please?"

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1 Could you take me to the Hilton Hotel, please? Poderia me levar ao Hotel Hilton, por favor?
2 How much is it to Manhattan? Quanto custa até Manhattan?
3 Could you drop me off here, please? Poderia me deixar aqui, por favor?
4 Go straight and turn left at the traffic light. Siga reto e vire à esquerda no semáforo.
5 Could I have a receipt, please? Posso ter um recibo, por favor?
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

At the Hotel -- Check-in

"I have a reservation under..." (stating a booking) + "Could I have/get...?" (polite requests) + "I would like..." (polite wants)
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Reservation -- a booking you make in advance
"I have a reservation for tonight." (Reserva)
Front Desk -- the reception area of a hotel
"Please go to the front desk to check in." (Recepção)
Key Card -- a plastic card to open your room door
"Here is your key card for room 712." (Cartão-chave)
Lobby -- the main entrance area of a hotel
"I am waiting in the lobby." (Saguão)
Floor -- a level in a building
"My room is on the seventh floor." (Andar)
Elevator -- a machine that moves between floors
"Where is the elevator?" (Elevador)
Bellhop -- a hotel worker who carries your bags
"The bellhop helped me with my bags." (Carregador)
Room Service -- food delivered to your room
"I would like to order room service, please." (Serviço de quarto)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition.

Reservation
Front Desk
Key Card
Lobby
Floor
Elevator
Bellhop
Room Service

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine arrives at the Grand Central Hotel in Manhattan. She walks into the lobby and goes to the front desk. "I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho," she says. The receptionist, Lisa, checks the computer. "Welcome, Ms. Pinho! Your room is on the seventh floor. Here is your key card," Lisa says.

"Could I have a room on a high floor, please?" Elaine asks. "Of course! Room 712. The elevator is on your right. I would like to send a bellhop to help with your bags," Lisa says. Elaine smiles. "Thank you! And later, I would like to order room service." She takes her key card and walks to the elevator, ready for her first night in New York.

1. How does Elaine tell the receptionist about her booking?
A "I booked room please."
B "I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho."
C "My name reservation."
2. How does Elaine make a polite request?
A "Give me high floor."
B "I want high floor now."
C "Could I have a room on a high floor, please?"
3. What does Elaine want to do later in her room?
A She would like to order room service.
B She wants to find the lobby.
C She needs a new key card.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
"I have a reservation under..." = informando sua reserva
EnglishPortuguês
I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho.Tenho uma reserva no nome de Elaine Pinho.
I have a reservation under the name Johnson.Tenho uma reserva no nome Johnson.
"Could I have/get...?" = pedido educado
EnglishPortuguês
Could I have extra towels, please?Poderia me dar toalhas extras, por favor?
Could I get a late checkout, please?Poderia ter um late checkout, por favor?
"I would like..." = querer educadamente
EnglishPortuguês
I would like a room on a high floor, please.Eu gostaria de um quarto em um andar alto, por favor.
I would like to order room service, please.Eu gostaria de pedir serviço de quarto, por favor.

Common Mistake:

"I want room high floor." ✗

"I would like a room on a high floor, please." ✓

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I have a under Elaine Pinho."
Please go to the to check in.
Here is your for room 712.
The helped me with my bags.
My room is on the seventh .
I would like to order , please.

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? You receive your key card and ask about the elevator.
? You arrive at the hotel and walk into the lobby.
? You enter your room and order room service.
? You go to the front desk and say your name.
? The bellhop takes your bags to your room.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho.
Could I have a key card for room 712, please?
I would like a room on a high floor, please.
I would like to order room service, please.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You arrive at the hotel and go to the front desk. How do you tell the receptionist about your booking?
A "I booked room. My name."
B "I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho."
C "Room please now."
You want extra towels in your room. How do you ask politely?
A "Give me towels."
B "I need towel more."
C "Could I have extra towels, please?"
You want to order food to your room. What do you say?
A "I would like to order room service, please."
B "Food my room now."
C "Bring food."
You cannot find the elevator. What do you ask at the front desk?
A "Elevator where?"
B "Where is the elevator?"
C "I go up."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you arrive at a hotel in New York. You need to check in at the front desk. Tell the receptionist: your name and reservation, ask for a room on a high floor, and ask where the elevator is. Try to use: "I have a reservation under...", "Could I have...?", and "I would like..."
Sugestão: "I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho. I would like a room on a high floor, please. Could I have extra towels? Where is the elevator?"

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1 I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho. Tenho uma reserva no nome de Elaine Pinho.
2 Could I have extra towels, please? Poderia me dar toalhas extras, por favor?
3 I would like to order room service, please. Eu gostaria de pedir serviço de quarto, por favor.
4 Where is the elevator? Onde fica o elevador?
5 Could I get a late checkout, please? Poderia ter um late checkout, por favor?
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Hotel Problems

"There is/are..." (describing problems) + "...is not working" (reporting broken things) + "Could you send someone to fix...?" (requesting help)
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

These are the key words for this lesson. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Towel -- a cloth for drying your body
"Could I get extra towels, please?" (Toalha)
Air Conditioning -- a machine that makes the room cool
"The air conditioning is not working." (Ar-condicionado)
Noise -- unwanted loud sounds
"There is a lot of noise from the room next door." (Barulho)
Broken -- not working, damaged
"The TV in my room is broken." (Quebrado)
Complaint -- telling someone that something is wrong
"I would like to make a complaint about the noise." (Reclamação)
Fix -- to repair something that is broken
"Could you send someone to fix it?" (Consertar)
Leak -- water coming through where it should not
"There is a leak in the bathroom." (Vazamento)
Manager -- the person in charge of the hotel
"I would like to speak with the manager, please." (Gerente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition.

Towel
Air Conditioning
Noise
Broken
Complaint
Fix
Leak
Manager

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine is in room 712. It is her first night in New York. She goes to the bathroom and sees there is a leak under the sink. Water is on the floor. She also notices the air conditioning is not working. The room is very hot. Then she hears noise from the room next door. It is very loud.

Elaine picks up the phone. "Hello, there is a problem with my room. The air conditioning is not working and there is a leak in the bathroom. Could you send someone to fix it?" The front desk agent says: "I am sorry, Ms. Pinho. I will send someone right away." Elaine also asks: "There are no towels in my bathroom. Could I get some, please?" She feels proud that she can explain the problems in English.

1. What structure does Elaine use to describe one problem?
A "It has a problem."
B "There is a leak in the bathroom."
C "Leak bathroom."
2. How does Elaine describe something that does not work?
A "Air conditioning no good."
B "It broken air."
C "The air conditioning is not working."
3. How does Elaine politely ask for help?
A "Could you send someone to fix it?"
B "Fix it now."
C "You fix please."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar
"There is..." = descrevendo UM problema
EnglishPortuguês
There is a leak in the bathroom.Tem um vazamento no banheiro.
There is a problem with the air conditioning.Tem um problema com o ar-condicionado.
There is noise from the room next door.Tem barulho do quarto ao lado.
"There are..." = descrevendo problemas NO PLURAL
EnglishPortuguês
There are no towels in the bathroom.Não tem toalhas no banheiro.
There are problems with my room.Tem problemas com meu quarto.
"...is not working" = algo que não funciona
EnglishPortuguês
The air conditioning is not working.O ar-condicionado não está funcionando.
The shower is not working.O chuveiro não está funcionando.
"Could you send someone to fix...?" = pedindo ajuda educadamente
EnglishPortuguês
Could you send someone to fix it?Você poderia mandar alguém consertar?
Could you send someone to fix the leak?Você poderia mandar alguém consertar o vazamento?

Common Mistake:

"It has a leak in the bathroom." ✗

"There is a leak in the bathroom." ✓

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

There is a in the bathroom.
The is not working in my room.
There are no in my bathroom.
Could you send someone to the leak?
I would like to make a .
I would like to speak with the , please.

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? The maintenance worker arrives and fixes the air conditioning.
? You notice the air conditioning is broken. Call the front desk.
? The maintenance worker fixes the leak and you thank him.
? You call the front desk and describe the problem.
? You tell the maintenance worker about the leak in the bathroom.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Compare word by word.

There is a problem with the air conditioning.
Could you send someone to fix it, please?
There is a leak in the bathroom. Could you fix it?
There are no towels in my room.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You arrive in your hotel room and the air conditioning is broken. How do you report this to the front desk?
A "Air broken. Fix now."
B "The air conditioning is not working. Could you send someone to fix it?"
C "No cold air room."
You go to the bathroom and there is water on the floor from a pipe. How do you describe this?
A "Water floor bathroom."
B "It has water problem."
C "There is a leak in the bathroom."
You need towels but there are none in your room. What do you say?
A "There are no towels in my room. Could I get some, please?"
B "Give towels."
C "Towel no have room."
The room next door is very loud at night. What do you say to the front desk?
A "Room loud. Stop it."
B "There is a lot of noise from the room next door. Could you do something about it?"
C "Noise room problem me."

Stage 5: Think and Speak

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you are in a hotel room. The air conditioning is not working, there is a leak in the bathroom, and there are no towels. Call the front desk and explain all the problems. Try to use: "There is...", "There are...", "...is not working", and "Could you send someone to fix...?"
Sugestão: "Hello, there is a problem with my room. The air conditioning is not working. There is also a leak in the bathroom. Could you send someone to fix it? And there are no towels. Could I get some, please?"

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1 There is a problem with my room. Tem um problema com meu quarto.
2 The air conditioning is not working. O ar-condicionado não está funcionando.
3 There is a leak in the bathroom. Tem um vazamento no banheiro.
4 Could you send someone to fix it? Você poderia mandar alguém consertar?
5 I would like to speak with the manager, please. Eu gostaria de falar com o gerente, por favor.
Aula 08 -- Pre-class (Review)

Review 1 -- Airport to Hotel

Full journey review: boarding pass, luggage, immigration, fare, reservation, key card, complaint, fix. All structures from Aulas 1-7.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

REVIEW

Review the grammar structures and examples.

Boarding Pass -- the card you need to get on the plane
"Could I see your boarding pass, please?" (Cartão de embarque)
You learned this in Lesson 2
Luggage -- bags and suitcases you take when you travel
"I took my luggage to the check-in counter." (Bagagem)
You learned this in Lesson 2
Immigration -- the place where officers check your passport
"The immigration officer checked my passport." (Imigração)
You learned this in Lesson 4
Fare -- the price you pay for a taxi ride
"The taxi fare was forty dollars." (Tarifa)
You learned this in Lesson 5
Reservation -- a booking you make in advance
"I have a reservation at the Grand Central Hotel." (Reserva)
You learned this in Lesson 6
Key Card -- a plastic card to open your room door
"Here is your key card for room 712." (Cartão do quarto)
You learned this in Lesson 6
Complaint -- telling someone that something is wrong
"I had a complaint about the air conditioning." (Reclamação)
You learned this in Lesson 7
Fix -- to repair something that is broken
"The maintenance worker came to fix the problem." (Consertar)
You learned this in Lesson 7

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its definition.

Fare
Key Card
Luggage
Complaint
Immigration
Boarding Pass
Fix
Reservation

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine lives in Indaiatuba. She travels alone. She arrives at the airport with her luggage. She shows her boarding pass and passport. Could I have a window seat, please? she asks. The officer at immigration checks her documents. How long are you staying? Five nights. She takes a taxi to the hotel. The fare is forty dollars. I would like to go to the Grand Central Hotel, please. At the hotel, she has a reservation. The receptionist gives her the key card. In the room, there is a problem. The air conditioning is not working. She makes a complaint. The maintenance worker comes to fix it.

1. Qual estrutura Elaine usa para pedir educadamente o assento na janela? / Which structure does Elaine use to politely ask for the window seat?
A I would like a window seat.
B Could I have a window seat, please?
C There is a window seat.
2. Qual estrutura Elaine usa para descrever o problema no quarto? / Which structure describes the problem in the room?
A I would like the air conditioning.
B Could you fix the air conditioning?
C The air conditioning is not working.
3. Para dizer que existe um problema, Elaine usa qual estrutura? / To say a problem exists, Elaine uses which structure?
A There is a problem.
B I have a problem.
C Could I have a problem?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Summary of All Structures

GRAMMAR

Resumo de TODAS as estruturas gramaticais das Aulas 1-7. / Summary of ALL grammar structures from Lessons 1-7.

Structure / Estrutura Use / Uso Example / Exemplo
Present Simple Falar de rotinas e fatos / Talk about routines and facts I travel alone. / She lives in Indaiatuba.
Could I...? / Could you...? Pedidos educados / Polite requests Could I have a window seat? / Could you send someone?
I would like... Desejos educados / Polite wants I would like to check in, please.
There is / There are Descrever situações e problemas / Describe situations and problems There is a problem. / There are no towels.
...is not working Reportar problemas / Report broken things The air conditioning is not working.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" a window seat, please?"
" to go to the Grand Central Hotel, please."
" a problem with the air conditioning."
"The shower ."
"She in Indaiatuba."
" send someone to fix it, please?"

Stage 2: Put the Journey in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? She takes a taxi to the hotel and pays the fare.
? She discovers a problem: the air conditioning is not working.
? She shows her boarding pass and checks in her luggage.
? The maintenance worker comes to fix the problem.
? Elaine arrives at the airport with her luggage.
? She checks in at the hotel and receives her key card.
? The immigration officer checks her passport.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique frases que misturam vocabulário de diferentes aulas.

I showed my boarding pass and walked to the gate.
The taxi fare from the airport to the hotel was forty dollars.
I have a reservation at the Grand Central Hotel.
There is a problem with the air conditioning. It is not working.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You arrive at the airport check-in counter. What do you say? / Você chega no balcão de check-in do aeroporto. O que você diz?
A "Good morning. I would like to check in for my flight."
B "There is a problem with my flight."
C "Could you fix my boarding pass?"
The immigration officer asks: "What is the purpose of your visit?" What do you say? / O oficial pergunta o motivo da visita. O que você diz?
A "I would like to go to the hotel."
B "I am here on vacation."
C "There is a problem with my passport."
You get to the hotel and the shower is broken. What do you say to the front desk? / O chuveiro do hotel está quebrado. O que você diz na recepção?
A "I would like a new shower."
B "There is a reservation for the shower."
C "The shower is not working. Could you send someone to fix it?"
You want to take a taxi to the hotel. What do you say to the driver? / Você quer ir de táxi ao hotel. O que você diz ao motorista?
A "There is a hotel near here."
B "I would like to go to the Grand Central Hotel, please."
C "Could I have a key card?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Record yourself telling the story of Elaine's journey from the airport to the hotel. Use as many words and structures as you can remember: boarding pass, luggage, immigration, fare, reservation, key card, complaint, fix, Could I...?, I would like..., There is/are..., ...is not working.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8 (Review)

1 I would like to check in for my flight, please. Eu gostaria de fazer o check-in do meu voo, por favor.
2 Could I have my boarding pass, please? Posso ter meu cartão de embarque, por favor?
3 I have a reservation under Elaine Pinho. Tenho uma reserva no nome de Elaine Pinho.
4 The air conditioning is not working. Could you send someone to fix it? O ar-condicionado não funciona. Pode mandar alguém consertar?
5 I would like to go to the Grand Central Hotel, please. Eu gostaria de ir ao Grand Central Hotel, por favor.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Restaurant Basics -- Ordering Food

Read a menu and order food and drinks politely: menu, waiter, appetizer, main course, dessert, the check, tip. Structures: "I would like..." and "Could I have...?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Menu -- the list of food and drinks you can order
"Could I have the menu, please?" (Cardápio)
Waiter -- the person who brings your food
"The waiter brings the menu." (Garçom)
Appetizer -- a small dish you eat before the main dish
"For the appetizer, I would like soup." (Entrada)
Main Course -- the biggest and most important dish
"For the main course, the chicken." (Prato principal)
Dessert -- something sweet you eat at the end
"For dessert, the chocolate cake." (Sobremesa)
The Check -- the paper that shows how much you pay
"Could I have the check, please?" (A conta)
Tip -- extra money you leave for good service
"I leave a tip for the waiter." (Gorjeta)
Order -- to ask for food in a restaurant
"I am ready to order." (Pedir)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Menu
Waiter
Appetizer
Main Course
Dessert
The Check
Tip
Order

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine arrives at the Grand Central Bistro. She has a reservation. The waiter brings the menu. Could I have a sparkling water, please? she asks. For the appetizer, I would like the tomato soup. For the main course, I would like the grilled chicken. At the end, she orders a chocolate cake for dessert. The food is delicious. Could I have the check, please? she says. She pays and leaves a tip for the waiter.

1. Qual estrutura Elaine usa para pedir o prato principal? / Which structure does Elaine use to order the main course?
A There is the grilled chicken.
B I would like the grilled chicken.
C Could you the grilled chicken?
2. Como Elaine pede a conta? / How does Elaine ask for the check?
A I would like the check.
B Could I have the check, please?
C There is the check.
3. O que Elaine deixa para o garçom no final? / What does Elaine leave for the waiter at the end?
A A tip.
B A menu.
C A reservation.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Two Ways to Order

GRAMMAR

Duas formas educadas de pedir em um restaurante. / Two polite ways to order in a restaurant.

Structure / Estrutura Use / Uso Example / Exemplo
I would like + noun Pedir comida e bebida / Order food and drinks I would like the soup, please.
Could I have + noun? Pedir o cardápio, a conta, água / Ask for the menu, the check, water Could I have the menu, please?
please Sempre no final, para ser educado / Always at the end, to be polite I would like a coffee, please.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" the menu, please?"
" the soup, please."
" the check, please?"
" something to drink?"
" a coffee, please."
"For dessert, the chocolate cake."

Stage 2: Put the Dinner in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

? She orders the grilled chicken for the main course.
? The waiter brings the menu.
? She asks for the check and leaves a tip.
? For the appetizer, she orders the tomato soup.
? For dessert, she orders the chocolate cake.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que você vai usar no restaurante.

Could I have the menu, please? I would like to order.
I would like the grilled chicken, please.
Could I have a sparkling water, please?
Could I have the check, please? Thank you very much.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

The waiter brings you to your table. You want to see the food. What do you say? / O garçom te leva à mesa. Você quer ver a comida. O que você diz?
A "Could I have the menu, please?"
B "There is a menu."
C "I am the menu."
The waiter asks: "Are you ready to order?" You want the soup. What do you say? / O garçom pergunta se você quer pedir. Você quer a sopa. O que você diz?
A "There is the soup."
B "I would like the tomato soup, please."
C "Could you the soup?"
You finished your dinner and you want to pay. What do you say? / Você terminou o jantar e quer pagar. O que você diz?
A "I would like to pay the menu."
B "There is the check."
C "Could I have the check, please?"
You want a glass of water. What is the most polite option? / Você quer um copo d'água. Qual é a opção mais educada?
A "Water."
B "Give me water."
C "Could I have a glass of water, please?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you are at a restaurant in New York. Order a full dinner: ask for the menu, order an appetizer, a main course, a drink and a dessert, then ask for the check. Use: Could I have...?, I would like..., appetizer, main course, dessert, the check.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1 Could I have the menu, please? Eu poderia ver o cardápio, por favor?
2 I would like to order, please. Eu gostaria de pedir, por favor.
3 For the main course, I would like the chicken. Para o prato principal, eu gostaria do frango.
4 Could I have the check, please? A conta, por favor?
5 Could I have a glass of water, please? Eu poderia tomar um copo d'água, por favor?
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Special Requests -- Allergies

Talk about food allergies and ask the waiter for changes: allergic, allergy, nuts, shellfish, gluten, dairy, spicy, ingredient. Structures: "I am allergic to...", "Can you...?" and "Does it have... in it?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Allergic -- when your body reacts badly to a food
"I am allergic to nuts." (Alérgico)
Allergy -- the bad reaction your body has to a food
"I have a food allergy." (Alergia)
Nuts -- small hard foods like peanuts and almonds
"Does the salad have nuts in it?" (Castanhas)
Shellfish -- sea food like shrimp and crab
"I am allergic to shellfish." (Frutos do mar)
Gluten -- a part of wheat, found in bread and pasta
"Is there gluten in this bread?" (Glúten)
Dairy -- food made from milk, like cheese and butter
"Can you make it without dairy?" (Laticínios)
Spicy -- food that is hot and burns your mouth, like chili
"Is this dish spicy?" (Picante)
Ingredient -- one of the foods used to make a dish
"What ingredients are in the soup?" (Ingrediente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Allergic
Allergy
Nuts
Shellfish
Gluten
Dairy
Spicy
Ingredient

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine is back at the Grand Central Bistro. Before she orders, she tells the waiter something important. I am allergic to nuts and I am allergic to shellfish, she says. She looks at the menu and asks, does the salad have nuts in it? Yes, says the waiter. Can you make it without nuts? she asks. Of course, he answers. For the main course, she asks for a dish without dairy. The waiter recommends the grilled chicken. It is not spicy. Now Elaine can eat safely.

1. Qual estrutura Elaine usa para falar da alergia? / Which structure does Elaine use to talk about her allergy?
A I have allergic of nuts.
B I am allergic to nuts.
C I am allergic for nuts.
2. Como Elaine pede uma mudança no prato? / How does Elaine ask for a change to the dish?
A You make it without nuts?
B Can you make it without nuts?
C Can you to make it without nuts?
3. Como Elaine pergunta sobre um ingrediente? / How does Elaine ask about an ingredient?
A Does the salad have nuts in it?
B The salad has nuts?
C Does the salad has nuts?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Allergies & Requests

GRAMMAR

Três estruturas para comer com segurança. / Three structures to eat safely.

Structure / Estrutura Use / Uso Example / Exemplo
I am allergic to + food Dizer qual comida é perigosa / Say which food is dangerous for you I am allergic to peanuts.
Can you + verb...? Pedir educadamente ao garçom / Ask the waiter politely to do something Can you make it without nuts?
Does it have + food + in it? Perguntar sobre um ingrediente / Ask about an ingredient Does it have gluten in it?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" allergic to nuts."
"Can you make it dairy?"
"Does it shellfish in it?"
" you make it without gluten?"
"I am allergic shellfish."
"Can you a dish without nuts?"

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

? She asks the waiter to make the salad without nuts.
? The waiter brings the menu.
? She orders a safe main course without dairy.
? She tells the waiter she is allergic to nuts.
? She asks if the salad has nuts in it.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que você vai usar no restaurante.

I am allergic to nuts. Does it have nuts in it?
Can you make it without dairy, please?
Can you recommend a dish without shellfish?
Is there gluten in this? I am allergic to gluten.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You cannot eat shellfish. How do you tell the waiter? / Você não pode comer frutos do mar. Como você conta ao garçom?
A "I am allergic to shellfish."
B "There is shellfish."
C "I am allergic of shellfish."
The pizza has cheese, but you cannot eat dairy. What do you say? / A pizza tem queijo, mas você não pode comer laticínios. O que você diz?
A "No cheese."
B "Can you make it without dairy, please?"
C "I would like dairy."
You want to know if the soup has gluten. What do you ask? / Você quer saber se a sopa tem glúten. O que você pergunta?
A "The soup has gluten?"
B "Does the soup has gluten?"
C "Does the soup have gluten in it?"
You don't like very hot food. What do you ask the waiter? / Você não gosta de comida muito apimentada. O que você pergunta ao garçom?
A "Is it allergic?"
B "Is this dish spicy?"
C "Is it a tip?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you are at a restaurant in New York. You have a food allergy. Tell the waiter your allergy, ask about an ingredient, and ask him to change a dish. Use: I am allergic to..., Does it have... in it?, Can you make it without...?, Can you recommend...?

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1 I am allergic to nuts. Eu sou alérgica a castanhas.
2 Does it have shellfish in it? Tem frutos do mar nisso?
3 Can you make it without dairy? Você pode fazer sem laticínios?
4 Is this dish spicy? Este prato é picante?
5 Can you recommend something without gluten? Você pode recomendar algo sem glúten?
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Paying the Bill

Ask for the bill, understand numbers, and pay politely: bill, cash, card, change, receipt, total, split, cents. Structures: "How much is it?", "Can I pay by card?", "Could I have the bill, please?" and "Can we split the bill?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Bill -- the paper that shows the money you must pay
"Could I have the bill, please?" (A conta)
Cash -- money in coins and paper notes
"Can I pay with cash?" (Dinheiro)
Card -- a small plastic card you use to pay
"Can I pay by card?" (Cartão)
Change -- the money you get back when you pay too much
"Keep the change." (Troco)
Receipt -- the paper proof that you paid
"Could I have a receipt, please?" (Recibo)
Total -- the full amount of money you must pay
"The total is forty-two dollars." (Total)
Split -- to divide the bill between people
"Can we split the bill?" (Dividir)
Cents -- small money: 100 of these make one dollar
"It is twelve dollars and fifty cents." (Centavos)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Bill
Cash
Card
Change
Receipt
Total
Split
Cents

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine finishes her dinner. The waiter comes and she says, Could I have the bill, please? The waiter brings the bill. The total is forty-two dollars. How much is the tip? she asks. About twenty percent, says the waiter. Can I pay by card? she asks. Yes, of course. She pays by card, asks for a receipt, and says, keep the change. The next day, at a cafe with a friend, they split the bill.

1. Como Elaine pede a conta? / How does Elaine ask for the bill?
A Bring me the bill.
B Could I have the bill, please?
C I want bill.
2. Como ela pergunta a forma de pagamento? / How does she ask how to pay?
A Can I pay with card?
B Can I pay by card?
C I pay card?
3. O que Elaine e a amiga fazem no café? / What do Elaine and her friend do at the cafe?
A They split the bill.
B They keep the menu.
C They order a reservation.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Asking & Paying

GRAMMAR

Três estruturas para pagar a conta. / Three structures to pay the bill.

Structure / Estrutura Use / Uso Example / Exemplo
How much is it? Perguntar o preço ou o total / Ask the price or total How much is it?
Can I pay by + card/cash? Perguntar como pagar / Ask how to pay Can I pay by card?
Could I have + the bill / a receipt? Pedir educadamente (revisão Aula 9) / Ask politely (review L9) Could I have the bill?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" the bill, please?"
"How much ?"
"Can I pay card?"
"Can we the bill?"
"Could I have a , please?"
"Keep the ."

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

? She asks if she can pay by card.
? Elaine asks the waiter for the bill.
? She asks for a receipt and says keep the change.
? The waiter brings the bill with the total.
? She pays by card and leaves a tip.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que você vai usar para pagar.

Could I have the bill, please?
How much is it? Can I pay by card?
Can we split the bill, please?
Could I have a receipt? Keep the change.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

Dinner is over. How do you ask the waiter to pay? / O jantar acabou. Como você pede para pagar?
A "Could I have the bill, please?"
B "I want the menu."
C "There is the bill."
You want to use your credit card. What do you ask? / Você quer usar o cartão. O que você pergunta?
A "Can I pay with card?"
B "Can I pay by card?"
C "I pay the card?"
You and a friend want to divide the bill. What do you say? / Você e uma amiga querem dividir a conta. O que você diz?
A "We cut the bill?"
B "Can we split the bill?"
C "Two bills, please."
The waiter can keep the small extra money. What do you say? / O garçom pode ficar com o troco. O que você diz?
A "Give me the change."
B "No receipt."
C "Keep the change."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you finished dinner at a restaurant in New York. Pay the bill: ask for the bill, ask the total, ask to pay by card, leave a tip, and ask for a receipt. Use: Could I have the bill?, How much is it?, Can I pay by card?, Keep the change.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1 Could I have the bill, please? Pode trazer a conta, por favor?
2 How much is it? Quanto é?
3 Can I pay by card? Posso pagar com cartão?
4 Can we split the bill? Podemos dividir a conta?
5 Could I have a receipt, please? Pode me dar um recibo, por favor?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Coffee Shops and Cafes

Order at a cafe and make small talk: barista, to go, refill, straw, napkin, muffin, decaf, mug. Structures: "Can I get...?", "For here or to go?", "I'll have..." and "How's your day going?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Barista -- the person who makes and serves the coffee
"The barista is very friendly." (Atendente do café)
To go -- when you take your drink outside, not inside
"A coffee to go, please." (Para viagem)
Refill -- more of the same drink, often for free
"Is the refill free?" (Reabastecer / refil)
Straw -- the thin tube you drink through
"Could I have a straw?" (Canudo)
Napkin -- the paper you use to clean your hands or mouth
"Could I have a napkin?" (Guardanapo)
Muffin -- a small sweet cake you eat with coffee
"I'll have a muffin, too." (Bolinho)
Decaf -- coffee with no caffeine
"Do you have decaf?" (Descafeinado)
Mug -- a big cup for a hot drink
"A mug of coffee, please." (Caneca)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Barista
To go
Refill
Straw
Napkin
Muffin
Decaf
Mug

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine goes into a coffee shop. The barista smiles and asks what she wants. Can I get a medium coffee, please? says Elaine. For here or to go? asks the barista. For here, please, says Elaine. She also says, I'll have a muffin, too. The barista asks if she wants a straw and a napkin. While she waits, they make small talk. How's your day going? Pretty good, thank you! It is a nice, sunny morning.

1. Como Elaine pede o cafe? / How does Elaine order the coffee?
A Give me a coffee.
B Can I get a medium coffee, please?
C I get coffee.
2. O que o barista pergunta? / What does the barista ask?
A For here or to go?
B Here or there go?
C You go here?
3. Qual e uma boa pergunta de small talk? / What is a good small talk question?
A How's your day going?
B How much your day?
C Where is your day?

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Ordering & Small Talk

GRAMMAR

Tres estruturas para o cafe. / Three structures for the cafe.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Can I get / I'll have + drinkPedir no cafe (casual) / Order at a cafeCan I get a coffee?
For here or to go?O barista pergunta onde voce vai beber / Where you drink itFor here, please.
How's your day going?Small talk amigavel / Friendly small talkPretty good, thank you!

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Can I a coffee, please?"
"For here, ."
"I'll a muffin, too."
"How's your day ?"
"Have a good !"
"Can I get a , please?"

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?The barista asks: for here or to go?
?Elaine goes into the coffee shop.
?They make small talk and Elaine pays.
?She asks for a coffee and a muffin.
?She answers: for here, please.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que voce vai usar no cafe.

Can I get a coffee, please? For here.
I'll have a muffin, too. Do you have decaf?
How's your day going? Nice weather, isn't it?
Have a good one!

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to order a coffee at a cafe. What do you say? / Voce quer pedir um cafe. O que voce diz?
A "Can I get a coffee, please?"
B "There is a coffee."
C "I am a coffee."
The barista asks "For here or to go?" You will take it outside. / Voce vai levar para fora.
A "For here."
B "To go, please."
C "No, thank you."
You want coffee without caffeine. What do you ask? / Voce quer cafe sem cafeina.
A "Do you have a refill?"
B "Do you have decaf?"
C "Do you have a straw?"
The barista is friendly. How do you make small talk? / Como voce faz small talk?
A "How's your day going?"
B "How much is your day?"
C "Where is your day?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you are in a coffee shop in New York. Order your coffee and a snack, answer "for here or to go?", and make small talk with the barista about the weather. Use: Can I get...?, I'll have..., for here, How's your day going?

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1Can I get a coffee, please?Pode me ver um cafe, por favor?
2For here, please.Para comer/beber aqui, por favor.
3How is your day going?Como esta o seu dia?
4Can I get a tea, please?Pode me ver um cha, por favor?
5Have a good one!Tenha um bom dia!
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Asking for Directions

Ask where a place is and follow directions: left, right, straight, corner, block, across from, next to, between. Structures: "Where is...?", "How do I get to...?", imperatives (Go/Turn) and prepositions of place.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Left -- the opposite of right
"Turn left at the corner." (Esquerda)
Right -- the opposite of left
"Turn right at the corner." (Direita)
Straight -- forward, not left and not right
"Go straight for two blocks." (Em frente / reto)
Corner -- where two streets meet
"It is on the corner." (Esquina)
Block -- the distance from one corner to the next
"Go two blocks." (Quarteirão)
Across from -- on the other side of the street, facing it
"It is across from the bank." (Em frente a / do outro lado)
Next to -- right beside something
"It is next to the park." (Ao lado de)
Between -- in the middle of two things
"It is between the bank and the store." (Entre)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Left
Right
Straight
Corner
Block
Across from
Next to
Between

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine wants to find the museum. She stops a man and asks, Where is the museum? It is not far, he says. Go straight for two blocks, then turn left at the corner. The museum is on your right, next to a big park. It is across from the bank. How do I get there? she asks. You can walk, it is five minutes. Elaine says thank you and finds the museum easily.

1. Como Elaine pergunta o lugar? / How does Elaine ask for the place?
A Where the museum is?
B Where is the museum?
C The museum where?
2. Qual e a direcao correta? / What is the correct direction?
A Turn to left.
B Turn left at the corner.
C Left turn you.
3. Onde fica o museu? / Where is the museum?
A Next to a big park, across from the bank.
B Inside the bank.
C Far from everything.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Asking & Giving Directions

GRAMMAR

Tres estruturas para se orientar. / Three structures to find your way.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Where is + place?Perguntar o lugar / Ask the locationWhere is the bank?
Go / Turn + directionDar direcao (imperativo) / Give directionsTurn left at the corner.
next to / across from / betweenDizer onde fica / Say where it isIt is next to the park.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" is the museum?"
"How do I to the station?"
"Go straight and left."
"It is the bank."
"Turn at the corner."
"Is it the bank?"

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?He says: go straight for two blocks.
?Elaine wants to find the museum.
?She says thank you and walks there.
?She asks a man: where is the museum?
?Turn left; the museum is next to the park.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que voce vai usar na rua.

Excuse me, where is the museum?
How do I get to the station?
Go straight and turn left at the corner.
It is next to the bank, across from the park.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You are lost and want the museum. What do you ask? / Voce esta perdida e quer o museu.
A "Excuse me, where is the museum?"
B "The museum is where?"
C "Museum, you?"
Someone asks the way. Tell them to go forward. / Diga para ir em frente.
A "You go to straight."
B "Go straight."
C "Straight going."
The bank is on the other side of the street. / O banco fica do outro lado da rua.
A "It is next the bank."
B "It is across from the bank."
C "It is between the bank."
You want to know how to reach the park. / Voce quer saber como chegar ao parque.
A "How I get to the park?"
B "How do I get to the park?"
C "Where I go park?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you are lost in New York. Stop a stranger and ask the way to a place. Then describe where your hotel is, using next to, across from, between. Use: Excuse me, where is...?, How do I get to...?, go straight, turn left/right.

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1Excuse me, where is the museum?Com licenca, onde fica o museu?
2How do I get to the station?Como eu chego na estacao?
3Go straight and turn left.Va em frente e vire a esquerda.
4Is it next to the bank?Fica ao lado do banco?
5Thank you for your help!Obrigada pela ajuda!
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Public Transportation

Use the subway and the bus: subway, bus, ticket, stop, platform, line, transfer, schedule. Structures: "Which... goes to...?", "How do I get to...?", "Where is the nearest...?" and "Do I need to...?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Subway -- the fast train under the ground in a city
"Take the subway to Times Square." (Metrô)
Bus -- a big road vehicle for many people
"Does this bus go to the park?" (Ônibus)
Ticket -- the paper or card you buy to travel
"How much is a ticket?" (Bilhete / passagem)
Stop -- the place where the bus or train waits for you
"It is four stops from here." (Parada)
Platform -- the long place where you wait for the train
"The platform is on your left." (Plataforma)
Line -- one route of the subway (red, blue...)
"The red line goes to Times Square." (Linha)
Transfer -- to change from one line to another
"Do I need to transfer?" (Baldeação / trocar)
Schedule -- the list of times when the train comes
"Check the schedule on the screen." (Horário)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Subway
Bus
Ticket
Stop
Platform
Line
Transfer
Schedule

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Elaine wants to go to Times Square. At the station she asks, Which subway goes to Times Square? The red line, says the clerk. A ticket is three dollars. Do I need to transfer? No, the line is direct. Elaine goes to the platform and waits. It is six stops. She watches the schedule on the screen. Soon the train arrives.

1. Como Elaine pergunta a linha? / How does Elaine ask about the line?
A Which subway go to Times Square?
B Which subway goes to Times Square?
C What subway is Times Square?
2. Ela precisa fazer baldeacao? / Does she need to transfer?
A No, the line is direct.
B Yes, three times.
C Yes, on the bus.
3. Quantas paradas ate Times Square? / How many stops to Times Square?
A Six stops.
B Sixty stops.
C No stops.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Asking About Transport

GRAMMAR

Tres estruturas para o transporte. / Three structures for transport.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Which + noun + goes to...?Escolher entre opcoes / Choose between optionsWhich bus goes to the park?
Where is the nearest + place?Achar o mais perto / Find the closestWhere is the nearest subway?
Do I need to + verb?Perguntar se e necessario / Ask if necessaryDo I need to transfer?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" subway goes to Times Square?"
"How do I to the airport?"
"Do I need to ?"
"Where is the subway?"
"Where is the ?"
"Which bus to the park?"

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She buys a ticket.
?Elaine wants to go to Times Square.
?The train arrives and she gets on.
?She asks which subway goes there.
?She goes down to the platform.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que voce vai usar no transporte.

Which subway goes to Times Square?
How much is a ticket?
Do I need to transfer?
Where is the platform?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
